metadata.title: Identifying the Context Shift between Test Benchmarks and Production Data

sections.title: Will your model work in production?

figures.description: ImageNet and CIFAR-10 accuracy trends over time for various models showing state-of-the-art progress
Scatter plots comparing new test accuracy vs original test accuracy to evaluate reproducibility and linear fit
Collage of four images showing model failures: cows on a beach, gender misclassification of Oprah Winfrey, a modified stop sign, and a crying child

sections.title: Contextual Dimensions in Human Centered ML Applications
content: skin color in face recognition (Buolamwini & Gebru 2018) and dermatology diagnosis (Groh et al 2021, Daneshjou et al, 2022), background scenery for affect recognition (Kosti et al, 2019), number of people in a video for deepfake detection (Groh et al 2022), number of chronic illnesses for algorithmic healthcare risk prediction (Obermeyer et al, 2019), data artifacts like surgical markings (Winkler et al 2019) or clinically irrelevant labels (Oakden-Rayner et al 2020) for medical diagnosis classification, and patients' demographics and self reports of pain for quantifying severity of knee osteoarthritis (Pierson et al, 2021)
Who is represented in the data? Who is annotating the data? When and where is data collected?How do social, geographical, temporal, aesthetic, financial, and other idiosyncrasies influence the data?

sections.title: Data Generation Process Desiderata for Dynamic Benchmarks
Case Study of Implicit Assumptions in Facial Expression Recognition
